_comment: Curation overlay for mlb-daily-dashboard. Sets first_delivery_outcome based on the user's explicit statement at ratification time that 'the instructions that came with the build failed, the build itself failed' on first delivery, followed by in-chat Phase 2 rectification to a working/promotable state.
_backfill_note: promoted_to / promoted_at / live_url / live_url_source were back-filled manually because workflow #2 run #20 (2026-05-31T03:30:48Z) failed at step 8 'Commit curation updates' due to a non-fast-forward push race with aggregator-on-push. The fork ceremony itself (step 7) succeeded — the fork repo, README, .claude/CLAUDE.md, cc-launch-prompt.md, and Pages were all created correctly. Only the curation push lost the race. Workflow fixed in the same commit cycle (retry-with-rebase loop in step 8) so future promotions don't hit this.
first_delivery_outcome_rationale: User explicitly stated at wrap-up time that on first delivery both halves of the first-contact experience failed: the install instructions did not work as written, and the runtime artifact itself did not satisfy the user. The build was rectified through Phase 2 in its original Cowork chat — iteration on the artifact until current requirements were satisfied. The install instructions on disk were deliberately not rewritten as part of the rectification (a user choice — they consider instruction state a frozen first-delivery data point, not a rectification target). Current state passes CV (verdict pass) and is in promotion condition, and Lane A at §2 wrap-up reflects that current lived experience. Per build-lifecycle.md the successful rectification does not erase the first-delivery failure; this overlay records it for the cross-corpus AutoBuilder process analysis the user explicitly called out as the reason this data matters.
notes: Source: user statement at ratification time, 2026-05-30, captured in runs/mlb-daily-dashboard/wrap-up-diagnosis.json under first_delivery_context. The wrap-up-diagnosis.json also surfaces an architectural amendment candidate: ratify-build.bat's two gates currently conflate first-delivery history with current satisfaction; mlb-daily-dashboard is the case that surfaced this. v1.x candidate for the interactive-wrapup spec.

revisions.summary: Phase 2 rectification in original Cowork chat — artifact iterated to working/promotable state after first-delivery failure of both install instructions and runtime artifact.
revisions.rationale: First delivery failed on both install path and runtime. User iterated in-chat until current build satisfied all original requirements. Install instructions on disk intentionally not rewritten — instruction state preserved as historical data point for AutoBuilder process analysis.
revisions.diff_summary: Iterative artifact fixes through Phase 2; instruction text unchanged from first delivery.
